content: We can frame general-purpose agent memory as a dynamic long-term retention system that uses hierarchical task-dependent memory compression, where episodic memories of low-level motion trajectories are incrementally summarized into higher-level task schemas during periods of agent inactivity (e.g., offline idle time), mirroring how human brains consolidate and compress redundant daily memories during sleep to preserve only action-relevant knowledge for future planning. This structured compression prioritizes high-value planning patterns over raw sensorimotor detail, allowing agents to scale to lifelong open-ended operation without overwhelming their memory storage.
source_topics: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
trigger_topic: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
